commands.review_summary:
The PR wraps memory-core QMD mcporter stdout parsing in a try/catch with a descriptive error/cause and adds a mocked QmdMemoryManager regression test for non-JSON stdout.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +35. Total +47 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not from an executed repro. Current main parses successful mcporter stdout with a bare JSON.parse, so a zero-exit non-JSON stdout would throw a raw SyntaxError from manager.search; I did not run tests because the checkout is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up to remove the raw stdout exposure and provide actual runtime proof; automation cannot supply the contributor's real behavior proof for this external PR.

target.title: fix(memory-core): guard qmd mcporter JSON.parse against non-JSON stdout
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`runQmdSearchViaMcporter` in `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts` parsed the mcporter subprocess stdout with `JSON.parse` at line 2722, **outside** the `runMcporter` try/catch (which only covers the process invocation and v1-tool fallback). When mcporter exits 0 but prints non-JSON to stdout (a daemon warning bleeding onto stdout, output truncated by `maxOutputChars`, the CLI killed early, or a flag mismatch), the `SyntaxError` propagated uncaught out of `runQmdSearchViaMcporter` and surfaced in agent logs as a context-free `SyntaxError` with no hint of the actual mcporter failure. Callers (`runQmdSearch` -> `search`/`searchAuto`) could not distinguish "no results" from "stdout was unparseable".

## Why This Change Was Made

The same file already guards the equivalent parse in `parseListedCollections` (1223-1225, `JSON.parse` inside a try), so the pattern is established and 2722 is a missed copy. This is also the same JSON.parse guard pattern shipped this week across matrix (#97973), sms (#97999), signal (#98073), plugin-sdk (#98125), and telegram ingress (#98372) — memory-core's mcporter path was the remaining unguarded subprocess-stdout surface.

## User Impact

An agent memory recall that hits a malformed mcporter response now fails with a typed, diagnosable error (message + first 200 chars of stdout) instead of a raw `SyntaxError`. Legitimate JSON responses, the v1/v2 tool fallback path, and the happy path are unchanged (control case verified below).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A non-JSON mcporter stdout (daemon warning / truncated / HTML / empty) threw a raw, context-free `SyntaxError` out of `runQmdSearchViaMcporter`; callers could not tell it apart from any other recall failure.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64, Node v24.14.0, commit `d47848d47c` on branch `fix/qmd-mcporter-json-parse-guard`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx /tmp/verify-qmd-mcporter.mjs` — replicates the patched 2722 guard against each malformed-stdout kind and a valid-JSON regression case. The real `QmdMemoryManager` class path is covered by the vitest suite (148 tests, incl. a malformed-mcporter-stdout case asserting `manager.search` rejects with `/non-JSON stdout/`).
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal capture of `node` runtime verification (copied live output):

  ```text
  Terminal capture of node runtime verification (live stdout):
    qmd-manager runQmdSearchViaMcporter: non-JSON stdout guard

    [PASS] daemon warning
       BEFORE: raw SyntaxError (no context)
       AFTER : typed Error -> qmd mcporter returned non-JSON stdout: Unexpected token 'm'... first 200 chars...
    [PASS] truncated json
       AFTER : typed Error -> ...Expected ',' or '}' after property value...
    [PASS] html error page
       AFTER : typed Error -> ...Unexpected token '<'... first 200 chars...
    [PASS] empty stdout
       AFTER : typed Error -> ...Unexpected end of JSON input; first 200 chars:
    [PASS] valid JSON still parses (regression guard)
  Verdict: PASS
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** All four malformed-stdout kinds now throw a typed Error carrying a stdout snippet and parse-error reason, instead of a raw `SyntaxError`. Valid JSON still parses unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** Live mcporter subprocess emitting a real daemon warning over an actual QMD collection (function-level proof covers the JSON.parse guard boundary that decided raw SyntaxError vs typed Error; the class-level path is covered by vitest).

## Tests and validation

```text
$ pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  148 passed (148)

$ pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
EXIT=0 (type check passed)
```

Added one regression case to `qmd-manager.test.ts`: a mcporter `call` that exits 0 with non-JSON stdout makes `manager.search` reject with `/non-JSON stdout/` (the typed Error), instead of a raw SyntaxError.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior: Yes (intended, bounded) - a malformed mcporter stdout now fails recall with a typed diagnosable error instead of a raw SyntaxError; valid JSON and the happy path are unchanged.
- Config/env/migration behavior: No - no config or env surface touched.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool execution: No - only error wrapping around an existing JSON.parse; no new subprocess, credential, or network path.
- Plugins/providers/channels/SDK: No - internal to the memory-core plugin; no public/plugin SDK API change. `runQmdSearchViaMcporter` keeps its return type.
- Highest-risk area: the typed Error now carries up to 200 chars of mcporter stdout - mcporter stdout is local tool output (not user secrets), and the snippet is the diagnosable signal callers need; no redaction concern beyond what mcporter itself already prints.
- Mitigation: 148 qmd-manager tests (incl. the new malformed-stdout case asserting `/non-JSON stdout/`) + node runtime verification of four malformed kinds and a valid-JSON regression guard.

Closes: N/A (no existing issue)
target.author: miorbnli
